seoTitle: How to Assign Tasks on Monday.com (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn to delegate work in Monday.com using the People column, multi-assignee tasks, automated notifications, and the My Work view.
intro: Unclear task ownership creates accountability gaps that slow projects down. This guide covers how to assign work in Monday.com using the People column, from adding individual or multiple owners to a task to setting up automatic notifications and tracking workload with the My Work panel and Workload widget.

steps.heading: Add the People column
steps.body: Locate the column directory on your master board and add the essential People Column.

steps.heading: Assign individual team members
steps.body: Assign individual team members, external guests, or entire pre-defined teams to specific task rows.

steps.heading: Enable multiple assignees
steps.body: Activate multi-person assignment settings for tasks that require collaborative steps.

steps.heading: Automate assignment notifications
steps.body: Set up automations to notify users via email, Slack, or internal alerts the moment a task is assigned to them.

steps.heading: Check the My Work view
steps.body: Use the My Work space for a personalized, aggregated view of every task assigned to you across all boards.

steps.heading: Track team workload
steps.body: Use the Workload Widget to monitor team bandwidth and manage resource allocation visually.

faq.question: Can I assign a task to more than one person?
faq.answer: Yes, you can activate multi-person assignment settings to allow multiple assignees on a collaborative task.

faq.question: Will people be notified when they're assigned a task?
faq.answer: Yes, low-code automations can send email, Slack, or internal alerts instantly when a task is assigned.

faq.question: Where can I see everything assigned to me?
faq.answer: The My Work space aggregates every task assigned to you across all your workspace boards in one view.

faq.question: Can I see if a team member is overloaded?
faq.answer: Yes, the Workload Widget visualizes team bandwidth so you can manage resource allocation before someone gets overloaded.
